bug_data.description:
When a proxy filter is registered, we now require speculative connection requests to provide a notification callback (see Bug 1943804). This breaks callers that don’t supply one ([example](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/16b037c409dcd2e6dca11e0ce61384bc52dbb78d/docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp#14038)).
This bug aims to ensure a callback is provided wherever possible.
bug_data.creator: kershaw@mozilla.com

crashes.signature: EMPTY: no frame data available; EmptyMinidump

bug_analysis.root_cause: Speculative connection requests without a notification callback are blocked when a proxy filter is registered, due to a new requirement introduced in Bug 1943804.
symptoms: Speculative connections fail silently or are blocked if no notification callback is provided
Breakage in callers that do not supply a notification callback, such as nsDocShell
reproduction_steps: Make a speculative connection request without providing a notification callback
Observe that the connection is blocked or fails
bug_analysis.technical_details: After Bug 1943804, speculative connection requests require a notification callback when a proxy filter is registered. Calls without such callbacks (e.g., in nsDocShell) are blocked. The fix ensures a callback is provided wherever possible to comply with this requirement.
bug_analysis.user_impact: Potential failures or delays in network speculative connections, possibly impacting page load performance or network behavior in Firefox.

common_patterns: Crashes occurring under memory pressure (OOM | small)
Crashes related to rendering or display list construction (Direct2D renderer, BuildDisplayListForChild, nsDisplayBackgroundImage)
Crashes triggered by resource-heavy operations (pretty printing large XML, background image rendering)
Unclear or no explicit resolution status (all listed bugs have Status: None)
potential_root_causes: Memory exhaustion or improper handling of memory limits during rendering and resource processing
Race conditions or improper sequencing when speculative connections or proxy filters interact with rendering pipeline
Resource management bugs in display list or background image handling under constrained conditions
relevant_fix_clues: No explicit fixes or resolutions indicated in the similar bugs
The original bug involves speculative connection blocking when proxy filter registered, hinting at network filter interactions affecting connection lifecycle
OOM-related crashes in rendering subsystems suggest that improving memory management or adding safeguards against out-of-memory conditions might help
similar_bugs_context.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes encountered in Firefox under memory pressure or during heavy rendering tasks often involve resource management issues in the rendering pipeline and network connection handling. The lack of resolved status in the related bugs indicates ongoing investigation, but the original bug's focus on speculative connection blocking with proxy filters hints at potential interplay between network and rendering subsystems causing instability. Addressing memory exhaustion and ensuring proper synchronization between network events and rendering operations could mitigate these crashes.

similar_bugs_data.summary: Direct2D renderer brightens images at certain zoom levels when window maximized, window margins altered, and small fonts and background image present on page
Thunderbird Windows crashes in OOM | small, even though Thunderbird is NOT out of memory per crash data
Crash when trying to pretty print 170MiB XML file
Crash in OOM | small with BuildDisplayListForChild

similar_bugs_analysis.summary: These similar bugs suggest that crashes encountered in Firefox under memory pressure or during heavy rendering tasks often involve resource management issues in the rendering pipeline and network connection handling. The lack of resolved status in the related bugs indicates ongoing investigation, but the original bug's focus on speculative connection blocking with proxy filters hints at potential interplay between network and rendering subsystems causing instability. Addressing memory exhaustion and ensuring proper synchronization between network events and rendering operations could mitigate these crashes.

refined_analysis.root_cause: After Bug 1943804, speculative connection requests without a notification callback are blocked if a proxy filter is registered. Speculative connect calls in docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp omit this callback, causing connection failures.
symptoms: Speculative connections silently fail or block when no notification callback is provided.
Page load delays and failures in nsDocShell.
reproduction_steps: Register a proxy filter using nsIProtocolProxyService.
Trigger a speculative connect from nsDocShell.cpp without a notification callback.
Observe connection blocking or silent failure.
refined_analysis.technical_details: Post Bug 1943804, speculative connects require a valid notification callback when a proxy filter is active; calls lacking it are blocked. nsDocShell.cpp must be updated to check for proxy filter presence and always pass a valid notification callback in speculative connect calls.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Speculative network connections fail or are delayed, causing degraded Firefox page load performance.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: In docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp, identify all speculative connect call sites and modify them to check for an active proxy filter. If present, ensure a valid notification callback is passed. Implement or reuse a suitable notification callback object to comply with the proxy filter requirement.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: Speculative connection requests issued from docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp fail silently or block after Bug 1943804 due to missing notification callbacks when a proxy filter is active. Update nsDocShell.cpp speculative connect invocations to always provide a valid notification callback after verifying proxy filter registration.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis correctly identifies the root cause, symptoms, and affected components but is overly verbose and contains some noise that dilutes focus. It lacks explicit mention of the exact functions or call sites in docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp that require modification, which reduces AI-readiness. The reproduction steps are clear but could be more concise. The implementation guidance is high-level and does not provide concrete code paths or an explicit fix sketch. The analysis is mostly self-contained but would benefit from trimming unrelated context and calling out precise artifact locations to improve scope and clarity.

filtered_context.concise_summary: After Bug 1943804, speculative connections from docshell/base/nsDocShell.cpp fail or block when no notification callback is provided and a proxy filter is registered. Update all speculative connect calls in nsDocShell.cpp to detect active proxy filters and always pass a valid notification callback to comply with the proxy filter requirements.
core_symptoms: Speculative connections silently fail or block without a notification callback when a proxy filter is active
Page load delays and failures in nsDocShell
technical_constraints: Speculative connect calls must include a valid notification callback if a proxy filter is registered
nsDocShell.cpp must check for proxy filter presence before issuing speculative connects
